methods.description: This method describes the growth conditions and treatment of Brassica napus canola cultivars and Arabidopsis for proteomic analysis.

steps.description: Brassica napus canola cultivars spp., compatible Westar and self-incompatible W1, were germinated on soil and maintained at standard growth conditions (22°C, 16 h/8 h day/night cycle).
steps.searchable_summary: germinate and grow Brassica napus under controlled conditions

steps.description: For proteomic analysis, W1 flowers were self-pollinated with W1 pollen for different times and pistils were harvested and frozen at -80°C until protein extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: self-pollinate W1 flowers and freeze pistils for proteomic analysis

steps.description: Arabidopsis Col-0 and mor1-1 were sterilized with 30% bleach for 10 min and germinated on half strength Murashige and Skoog basal salt mixture pH 5.7, grown for a week, transplanted on to soil and maintained at standard growth conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: sterilize and germinate Arabidopsis seeds, then grow under controlled conditions

steps.description: For restrictive temperature treatments (29°C), Col-0 and mor1-1 flowers were emasculated and moved to chambers preconditioned at 29°C followed by pollination after 12 h.
steps.searchable_summary: emasculate and pollinate Arabidopsis flowers under restrictive temperature

methods.method_name: Extraction of Total Proteins for 2-D DIGE
methods.description: This method outlines the extraction of total proteins from plant tissues using the phenol-methanol method for 2-D DIGE analysis.

steps.description: Total proteins were extracted using the phenol-methanol method.
steps.searchable_summary: extract proteins using phenol-methanol method

steps.description: For each biological replicate, ~100 stigmas were ground in liquid nitrogen with a pestle and mortar, followed by addition of 1 ml of protein extraction buffer and centrifugation at 15,000 x g for 20 min at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: grind stigmas and extract proteins with buffer

steps.description: The supernatant was mixed with equal volume of ice-cold phenol and spun down to separate phenol.
steps.searchable_summary: mix supernatant with phenol and centrifuge

steps.description: Following removal of the aqueous phase, the intact interface along with the phenol phase was extracted twice with the extraction buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract phenol phase with buffer

steps.description: To the interface and phenol phase, 5 volumes of cold 0.1 M ammonium acetate in methanol was added and incubated at -20°C overnight, followed by centrifugation.
steps.searchable_summary: precipitate proteins with ammonium acetate and methanol

steps.description: The supernatant was discarded and the pellet was washed twice with 80% acetone, allowed to dry and resuspended in lysis buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: wash protein pellet with acetone and resuspend

steps.description: Protein concentration determined using the Bio-Rad protein assay mixture using BSA as a standard.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein concentration with Bio-Rad assay

methods.description: This method describes the labeling of proteins with CyDye DIGE Fluor minimal dye for differential analysis.

steps.description: Proteins were labeled with CyDye DIGE Fluor minimal dye according to the manufacturer's instructions with modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: label proteins with CyDye

steps.description: 10 µl (around 3 µg/µl) of proteins were mixed with 0.2 µl 1.5 M Tris-HCl buffer to adjust the pH to around 8.8.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust protein solution pH with Tris-HCl

steps.description: To the protein solution, 100 pmol CyDye was added and incubated on ice in the dark for at least 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate protein solution with CyDye

steps.description: The reaction was stopped by addition of 0.5 µl of 10 mM lysine and incubated on ice for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: stop CyDye reaction with lysine

methods.description: This method describes the process of separating proteins using two-dimensional gel electrophoresis.

steps.description: Pairs of Cy3-and Cy5-labeled protein samples were mixed together.
steps.searchable_summary: mix Cy3 and Cy5 labeled proteins

steps.description: The DIGE sample buffer was added to bring the volume to 450 µl, and the samples were then applied to 24-cm Immobiline Drystrips.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare protein samples for IEF

steps.description: IEF was carried out on an Ettan IPGphor II at 20°C with maximum 50 µA/strip and specific voltage settings.
steps.searchable_summary: perform IEF on protein samples

steps.description: After IEF, IPG strips were equilibrated in equilibration buffer first with 0.5% dithiothreitol and then with 2% iodoacetamide each for 15 min.
steps.searchable_summary: equilibrate IPG strips with buffers

steps.description: The strips were then transferred to 10% SDS-PAGE gels for the second dimension electrophoresis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform second dimension electrophoresis

methods.description: This method involves scanning the gels to capture images of the separated proteins.

steps.description: The gels were scanned using a Typhoon 8600 scanner.
steps.searchable_summary: scan gels with Typhoon 8600

steps.description: Cy3-labeled sample was scanned using a 532 nm laser and a 580/30-nm band pass emission filter.
steps.searchable_summary: scan Cy3-labeled sample with specific laser and filter

steps.description: Cy5-labeled sample was scanned with a 633 nm laser and 670/30-nm band pass emission filter.
steps.searchable_summary: scan Cy5-labeled sample with specific laser and filter

steps.description: Post-electrophoretic staining with deep purple fluorescent dyes was performed and subsequent scanning was performed using the same setting as that used for Cy3-labeled sample.
steps.searchable_summary: stain and scan gels with deep purple dye

methods.description: This method describes the analysis of 2-D DIGE images using DeCyder software to identify differentially expressed protein spots.

steps.description: The DIGE images were analyzed using DeCyder version 6.5 software.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze DIGE images with DeCyder software

steps.description: The differential in-gel analysis module was used for spot detection, spot volume quantification, and volume ratio normalization.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spot detection and quantification

steps.description: The biological variation analysis module was used to match protein spots among different gels and to identify protein spots that exhibited significant difference.
steps.searchable_summary: match protein spots and identify significant differences

steps.description: Manual editing was performed to ensure that spots were correctly matched and not contaminated with artifacts.
steps.searchable_summary: manually edit spot data for accuracy

steps.description: Differentially expressed spots were selected based on the p value of t test (p < 0.05) and other criteria.
steps.searchable_summary: select differentially expressed spots based on statistical criteria

methods.description: This method involves selecting and picking protein spots of interest from gels for further analysis.

steps.description: Approximately 30 µg of protein were labeled with 50 pmol of Cy5 dye and mixed with 500 µg of unlabeled protein.
steps.searchable_summary: label and mix proteins for spot picking

steps.description: Spots of interests were selected with DeCyder software and picked by an Ettan spot picker.
steps.searchable_summary: select and pick protein spots with software and picker

methods.method_name: In-gel Tryptic Digestion, Mass Spectrometry, and Database Searching
methods.description: This method describes the process of digesting protein spots, analyzing them with mass spectrometry, and searching databases for protein identification.

steps.description: Samples were received as excised spots from a two-dimensional polyacrylamide gel and were reduced, alkylated, tryptically digested and extracted.
steps.searchable_summary: digest and extract proteins from gel spots

steps.description: Extracted peptides were desalted and concentrated using Millipore C18 zip-tips.
steps.searchable_summary: desalt and concentrate peptides

steps.description: Peptides were spotted onto a MALDI target plate and analyzed with a QSTAR XL QqTOF mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze peptides with mass spectrometer

steps.description: Peak lists were searched against the NCBI non-redundant database using Mascot software.
steps.searchable_summary: search peak lists in database with Mascot

steps.description: Identifications were subjected to BLAST analysis against the Arabidopsis protein database to find the closest homolog.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST analysis for protein homologs
